title: Car Pooling
notes: Time complexity is O(N log N): N increases by 10x per scale. From N=1k to N=10k, time increases by ~11.6x. From N=10k to N=100k, time increases by ~15.7x, showing slightly super-linear O(N log N) scaling due to sorting and heap operations. Space complexity is O(N) as peak memory increases by exactly ~10x for each 10x increase in N, reflecting the space used by the heap which can store up to N elements.
